name: Ran Brosh
title: Research Assistant Professor, Associate Director
lab_synopsis:
I have two roles: [1] as Faculty at ISG/Pathology I am leading the Dark Matter Project, a collaborative effort to use synthetic biology and genome writing to study the noncoding genome and its roles in health and disease. We develop method to build and deliver large DNA constructs to mammalian cells (primarily mouse embryonic stem cells) and apply them to generate mouse models for human diseases or in vitro cell models to study basic aspects of transcriptional regulation. [2] I'm the Associate Director of the Rodent Genetic Engineering Laboratory (RGEL), a core facility that generate transgenic mice by a variety of advanced methods.

Builds and applies genome writing platforms to rewrite large mammalian loci and model how regulatory DNA and transposable element insertions shape gene expression and disease phenotypes. Develops landing pad and large payload integration approaches such as Big IN and mSwAP In for iterative locus replacement in stem cells, enabling synthetic regulatory genomics studies including Sox2 locus control region dissection, ACE2 humanization for viral susceptibility models, and TAF1 humanization to test consequences of X linked dystonia parkinsonism associated SVA insertions. Supports end to end engineering workflows spanning design, assembly, validation, mouse model generation, and colony services.
common_experiments_raw:
*Mammalian genome writing
*Big-IN (genome writing)
*mSwAP-In (genome writing)
*CREEPY (CRSIPR editing of episomes in yeast)
DNA assembly
Synthetic biology
CRISPR/Cas9
PCR, qRT-PCR
RNA-seq
Illumina sequencing
Nanopore sequencing
Blastocyst injection
Pronuclear microinjection
In vitro fertilization
Rederivation
Sperm and embryo cryopreservation
experimental_capabilities: ChIP based profiling at engineered loci
capture seq for integration integrity and off target assessment
engineering of human embryonic stem cells and mouse embryonic stem cells
generation of humanized mouse alleles and transgenic models
iterative genome rewriting in mouse embryonic stem cells
mouse colony support workflows including IVF and rederivation
targeted integration of large DNA constructs
yeast based engineering of large episomal DNA constructs
